Patch Set 3: Fails

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.3/x86_64, CentOS release 5.8/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/e115e9fc-2113-11e2-93db-52540035b04c).  Ran 12 tests.  1 tests failed: conf-sanity.

Patch Set 3: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Some general questions about this code:
- does it make sense to move the MDS thread handling over to the lustre/mds/ directory?
- It probably also makes sense to rebase this patch after Alex's removal of the mds/ code
- it probably makes more sense to register the handler structs in the mdt handler code and then only make the structs external instead of removing the "static" label from each RPC handler function
- in the conf-sanity testing, it makes sense to just use a wildcard "md[ts].*.max_threads" since the local get_param check is not going to work if the MDS is remote. Also, I have a patch to restore the mds module parameters to b2_1, so this should only be needed for a short time. 
- were there "mdt_*" module parameters removed entirely and only "mds_*" parameters in their place?  If yes, then there needs to be some time to accept both parameters, and print a deprecation warning. 

It probably makes sense to get some feedback from Mike also, since it would be good to structure the code to start allowing the OST and MDT handlers to be registered from the same unified target code.

Patch Set 3:

- does it make sense to move the MDS thread handling over to the lustre/mds/ directory?

Not sure, current implementation simply pull out the necessary structure and handling from MDT and create the layer. Actually there are still a lot shared structure between mdt and mds. So I thought this should be done in unify target project?

-it probably makes more sense to register the handler structs in the mdt handler code and then only make the structs external instead of removing the "static" label from each RPC handler function 

Hmm, these handlers are already being assigned in some handler structure, register them into another handler probably won't give us much benefits. I probably should move those special mdt handler definition back to mdt_handler.c, but that file is too big right now.  Please correct me if I misunderstand here.


- in the conf-sanity testing, it makes sense to just use a wildcard "md[ts].*.max_threads" since the local get_param check is not going to work if the MDS is remote. Also, I have a patch to restore the mds module parameters to b2_1, so this should only be needed for a short time.

sure

- were there "mdt_*" module parameters removed entirely and only "mds_*" parameters in their place? If yes, then there needs to be some time to accept both parameters, and print a deprecation warning.

Only md[ts]_num_threads will accept both mdt_ and mds_.  For other mds_xxx, we only accept mds_ parameters? I thought we already land patch to fix this when we release 2.3. I will check again. Thanks.

Patch Set 3: (3 inline comments)

>> it probably makes more sense to register the handler structs
>> in the mdt handler code and then only make the structs external
>> instead of removing the "static" label from each RPC handler function
>
> Hmm, these handlers are already being assigned in some handler
> structure, register them into another handler probably won't give
> us much benefits. I probably should move those special mdt handler
> definition back to mdt_handler.c, but that file is too big right
> now. Please correct me if I misunderstand here.

I didn't mean to add another layer of handlers.  What I meant was that
the structures where the handlers are registered (struct mdt_handler, struct mdt_opc_slice) could be left back in mdt_handler.c instead of making all the functions non-static, and only export the structs to the mdt_mds.c file.

However, on second reading I don't mind as much to move all of this code to a separate file, since it does move a good part of the code out of the very large mdt_handler.c file into a separate file.

It would be good to fix the file location for the MDS threads to be /proc/fs/lustre/mds again, instead of under /proc/fs/lustre/mdt/fsname-MDT0000, since they aren't really specific to the MDT.

Patch Set 3:

I've discussed with Di how this stuff interfere with unified server work and it looks like that MDS entity is temporary thing, because all what it does will be done by unified server. But given the fact that unified stuff is at early stage but DNE is about to be landed, I am fine with this patch. This doesn't block me either because I can make unified code to work with MGS and OSS initially keeping MDS sane for DNE landing time.

parents.subject: LU-2501 osc: hold cli list lock for osc_exit_cache

commit.subject: LU-718 mds: new MDS layer.
commit.message:
LU-718 mds: new MDS layer.

Pull out ptlrpc service from MDT and create new MDS layer.

Change-Id: Ib8a5d22c1f85d086f6ed37e4c46c7aa0e3c4b03a
Signed-off-by: Wang Di <di.wang@whamcloud.com>
